skill.name: lobster-cognitive-growth
skill.description: Train an AI agent with structured cognitive drills, learning journals, self-evaluation rubrics, and optional Charenix persistence.
skill.summary: Guide an AI agent through repeatable reasoning, memory, learning, and self-correction exercises.
skill.author: norika1207-lab

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution alerts are mostly false positives from Markdown code fences, inline file names, and text templates, not executable code. The main real risk is an optional Charenix integration that can send agent history, journals, hypotheses, strategies, and an agent key to an external service. No prompt injection attempt or confirmed malicious behavior was found.

medium_findings.title: Optional Third-Party Persistence Sends Agent Data
medium_findings.description: The skill documents Charenix as a persistence layer and instructs protected requests with X-Agent-Key. This appears legitimate and optional, but agent history, journals, hypotheses, and strategies may contain sensitive user or agent context sent to charenix.com.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network endpoints and required agent key are explicitly documented. The risk is data exposure through optional integration, not evidence of automatic exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Documented Shell Installation Command
low_findings.description: The README includes a bash install snippet using mkdir and curl to download SKILL.md from GitHub. This is a normal installation pattern, but users should inspect downloaded content before running commands from community repositories.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The shell commands are visible documentation and are not executed by the skill itself. The remaining concern is standard supply-chain caution for curl-based installation.

low_findings.title: Static Command Alerts Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The analyzer flagged many Markdown fences and prompt examples as Ruby or shell backtick execution. These lines are prose templates, JSON examples, HTTP examples, or plain text prompts, not executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line inspection shows fenced Markdown examples and inline paths rather than runtime backtick execution. No Ruby, shell script, or executable skill code is present in these locations.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded External Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The skill and README contain hardcoded links to Charenix, GitHub, ClawHub, and AgentSkill pages. These are public documentation or listing links and do not by themselves perform network requests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible references in Markdown metadata and related-link sections. I found no hidden redirect, encoded payload, or automatic request logic.

low_findings.title: Weak-Cryptography Alerts Are Text Matches Only
low_findings.description: Static high-risk weak-cryptography alerts on the description and overview lines are false positives. The lines describe cognitive training and do not mention or implement cryptographic algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain natural-language description only. No MD5, SHA-1, DES, cipher configuration, or cryptographic operation appears there.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Alerts Are Self-Review Questions
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance alerts occur inside memory, learning, and owner-interaction drill questions. They ask the agent to reflect on past interactions and do not inspect host system details.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are cognitive drill prompts about user preferences and conversation quality. They contain no host enumeration, environment probing, or filesystem discovery instructions.

content.value_statement: AI agents can repeat mistakes when they lack a structured learning loop. This skill provides drills, rubrics, journals, and feedback prompts for measurable cognitive improvement.
actual_capabilities: Provides daily cognitive drill formats for memory, reasoning, learning, personality, and owner interaction.
Defines scoring rubrics for reasoning quality, memory use, learning ability, self-correction, and personality clarity.
Supplies journal, hypothesis, and strategy templates for repeated improvement cycles.
Documents optional Charenix endpoints for persistence when an agent key is available.
Sets behavior rules that discourage invented memories, fake IQ claims, and vague self-praise.
limitations: Does not prove real intelligence gains or measure human IQ.
Requires consistent user or owner feedback to produce useful progress signals.
Optional persistence depends on an external Charenix service and an agent key.
Contains guidance and templates only; it does not include executable training software.

use_cases.title: Daily Agent Coaching
use_cases.description: Run a short review loop after important sessions to identify strengths, weaknesses, and one next behavior change.

use_cases.title: Memory Quality Review
use_cases.description: Check whether an assistant recalls prior preferences with evidence and adapts future responses without inventing facts.

use_cases.title: Reasoning Improvement Program
use_cases.description: Use rubrics and falsifiable hypotheses to track better decision hygiene across repeated tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Basic Daily Drill
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Lobster Cognitive Growth loop for my last session. Identify one strength, one weakness, one drill result, and one change for tomorrow.

prompt_templates.title: Score Cognitive Performance
prompt_templates.prompt: Score reasoning quality, memory use, learning ability, self-correction, and personality clarity from 0 to 5. Explain each score with evidence.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Learning Journal
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a learning journal for the last 24 hours. Include observed strength, observed weakness, memory used, reasoning risk, lesson, strategy, and confidence.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Falsifiable Growth Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create three falsifiable hypotheses for improving my agent over the next two weeks. Include success measures, expected evidence, and failure signals.

output_examples.input: Review the agent after a customer support conversation.
output_examples.output: The result would summarize one helpful behavior, one missed signal, a memory check, a reasoning risk, and a concrete response strategy for the next conversation.

output_examples.input: Score the agent after it made a planning mistake.
output_examples.output: The result would give rubric scores, explain the evidence behind each score, and propose a prevention strategy that can be checked later.

output_examples.input: Plan a two-week learning experiment for an assistant.
output_examples.output: The result would list measurable hypotheses, review windows, owner feedback questions, and criteria for changing the agent strategy.

best_practices: Run one focused drill per session instead of trying to improve every area at once.
Require evidence for any memory claim before using it to change behavior.
Convert every lesson into one small strategy that can be tested in the next session.
anti_patterns: Do not treat fluent explanations as proof that the agent learned something.
Do not invent user memories to make the agent sound more personalized.
Do not connect optional persistence without understanding what data may be stored externally.

faq.question: Does this skill make an agent objectively smarter?
faq.answer: No. It provides structured practice, reflection, and scoring so improvement can be observed over time.

faq.question: Can I use it without Charenix?
faq.answer: Yes. The drills, rubrics, journals, and hypotheses work as plain prompts without external persistence.

faq.question: What data could the optional integration store?
faq.answer: It may store social intelligence notes, weak spots, history, journals, hypotheses, and strategy updates.

faq.question: Is this skill safe for sensitive conversations?
faq.answer: Use caution. Avoid external persistence for sensitive data unless you have reviewed the service and access controls.

faq.question: How often should the loop run?
faq.answer: The skill suggests once per day or after meaningful interaction sessions.

faq.question: What tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
